mechanism: ATP-competitive inhibitor of FLT3 kinase (inhibits FLT3-ITD signaling)
additional_notes: Clinical FLT3 inhibitor; active in FLT3-ITD models like MOLM13.

mechanism: Potent FLT3 kinase inhibitor (inhibits FLT3-ITD-driven proliferation)
additional_notes: Also known as AC220; highly potent in FLT3-ITD lines.

mechanism: Inhibits FLT3 and other kinases (PKC family), blocking FLT3-driven signaling
additional_notes: Approved for FLT3-mutant AML (in combination therapy).

mechanism: Inhibits FLT3 (including some resistant alleles) and PDGFR family kinases
additional_notes: Activity against FLT3-ITD and certain resistance mutations.

mechanism: Inhibits RAF kinases and receptor tyrosine kinases (VEGFR, PDGFR) and has FLT3 activity
additional_notes: Used off-label in FLT3 AML; broad kinase profile.

mechanism: Inhibits BCR-ABL and multiple tyrosine kinases including FLT3
additional_notes: Reported activity in FLT3 AML models.

mechanism: Selective BH3-mimetic inhibitor of BCL-2, induces apoptosis via mitochondrial pathway
additional_notes: Synergizes with hypomethylating agents (azacitidine/decitabine) in AML.

mechanism: Reversible inhibitor of the 26S proteasome (inhibits proteasomal degradation, inducing apoptosis)
additional_notes: Boronate proteasome inhibitor; anti-proliferative in AML models (top conc 0.1 µM = 100 nM).

mechanism: Irreversible epoxyketone inhibitor of the 20S proteasome (induces proteotoxic stress and apoptosis)
additional_notes: Potent proteasome inhibitor with low-nM activity in many cell types.

mechanism: Inhibits histone deacetylases, altering chromatin and gene expression to induce cell cycle arrest/apoptosis
additional_notes: Pan-HDAC activity; epigenetic modulator with in vitro AML activity.

mechanism: Broad inhibition of HDAC enzymes leading to increased histone acetylation and apoptosis
additional_notes: More potent pan-HDAC inhibitor than some class-selective agents; active in AML preclinical studies.

mechanism: Inhibits CDKs (notably CDK9), suppressing transcriptional elongation (e.g., MCL1 downregulation) and inducing apoptosis
additional_notes: Also known as flavopiridol; clinical history in AML trials.

mechanism: Inhibits Aurora A kinase, disrupting mitotic progression and inducing mitotic catastrophe
additional_notes: Also called MLN8237; mitotic kinase inhibitor with AML activity.

mechanism: Incorporates into RNA/DNA and inhibits DNA methyltransferases, causing hypomethylation and cytotoxicity in dividing cells
additional_notes: Standard AML therapy; incorporation-dependent effects—consider 96 h exposure for in vitro assays.

mechanism: Incorporates into DNA and irreversibly inhibits DNA methyltransferases leading to hypomethylation and cytotoxicity
additional_notes: Similar to azacitidine; incorporation-dependent—longer exposures may be needed in vitro.

mechanism: Inhibits mitochondrial complex I and alters cellular metabolism/AMPK signaling (anticancer effects often at high concentrations in vitro)
additional_notes: Often active only at high µM–mM ranges in vitro; top conc 20 mM. Prepare stocks appropriately; interpret with caution.

mechanism: Binds tubulin, disrupting microtubule polymerization and mitosis; reported anticancer/tubulin-target effects
additional_notes: Repurposing candidate with reported activity across several cancer models including AML.

mechanism: Antifungal (lanosterol 14α-demethylase inhibitor) with reported off-target effects on Hedgehog, PI3K/mTOR and angiogenesis pathways in cancer models
additional_notes: Repurposing candidate with multiple reported anticancer mechanisms and known CYP3A4 interactions.

mechanism: ALDH inhibitor; forms copper complexes that generate ROS and can inhibit proteasome/induce cytotoxicity (copper-dependent anticancer activity)
additional_notes: Run assays with CuCl2 (1 µM final) as indicated; include parallel control without copper. Antialcoholism drug with reported anticancer effects when complexed with copper.

mechanism: Generates reactive oxygen species via endoperoxide activation (iron-dependent), causing DNA/protein damage and cell death
additional_notes: Artemisinin-class compound with reported low-to-mid µM activity in AML lines.
